title: Level 435
wikitext:
{{CSS|content= .cover2 { opacity: .8; position: fixed; width: 100vw; height: 100vh; top: 0; right: 50px; z-index: 99999; bottom: 0; left: 0; margin: 0; pointer-events: none; user-select: none; } .cover2 img { width: 100%; height: 100%; object-fit: cover; } {{Rewrite|This page is annoyingly mid|date=Friday, January 16th 2026}} }} __NOTOC__ {{Background|file=Cool room.webp}}
{{Audio|file=dark ambience.mp3|secrettext=hi|volume=0.1}} '''Date unknown – time is meaningless here



I’ve been wandering this endless field for what feels like days now, the sun is dark, and all I could see was the light of a nonexistent moon. Hunger gnawed at my stomach, my legs felt like fragile straw, threatening to break then and there. Then I saw it. {{Blurimage|file=Screenshot 2025-11-28 124828.png|caption=the photo stitched to the book}}
Among the endless fields of cold grass, there was a castle.. or a church? It looked worn down, the stone cracked and decayed. I hesitated. Things in this twisted world aren't always as they seem; sometimes salvation can turn out to be a death trap. But I had no choice; if I stayed here longer and ignored my possibly only chance of survival, I'd surely die. I went up to the two huge doors, using up all my strength, I opened it. It was so dark I could barely see anything, I hesitated. I stepped through anyway. The doors slammed shut behind me. I turned— They were gone. The wall behind me was solid, smooth, unbroken. I don’t know where I am now, but it’s not that field anymore. The smell of the cold air was gone, replaced with rough, hard stone resembling Level 8. I don't feel relieved, I might have just thrown myself into another predicament... I’ll write again if I can.
-A journal entry made by an anonymous wanderer, status unknown.

|final=|body=}} {{Class|p3=5|p2=5|p1=4|level=435}} {{VoidedTheme}} ==Description== {{Blurimage ALT|file=Screenshot 2025-11-28 122948.png|caption=An Image of Level 435, taken by TheWanderer960. Wanderer not affiliated with any groups}}
Level 435, nicknamed "Cramped Passageways", is a level that manifests as an endless maze of claustrophobic corridors and variously sized caverns, many of which seem to defy natural geometry. Corridors stretch to impossible lengths or curve in illogical loops that lead wanderers back to previously visited areas, creating a sense of spatial distortion. Most of the level is flooded, with the pools of water being 10 to 15 meters deep, they normally contain the [[Hydrolitis Plague]] as well as [[Sanguine Festivus|SFV.]] The level typically alternates between wide open areas with staglamites littering the ceiling and cracked stone with rusty metal beams hanging loose. As a result of Due to this, the level is prone to Phenomenon 24.the darkness, falling into pits of water full of the Hydrolitis plague is common. Due to this environmental hazard, it is not recommended to traverse this level without a light source. Rooms connected to these corridors vary in size and function. Some resemble open caves similar to those in Level 8, containing staglamites, giant pits, or caverns. Sometimes, crates can be found containing supplies such as: [[Object 1|almond water]], canned goods, medical kits, batteries, and flashlights—though these are scarce and often expired or damaged. It is crucial to approach any crate cautiously, listening intently for growls, scratching, or whispering nearby before approaching the crate, as they can sometimes be traps made by entities. Temperature within the level fluctuates without warning. Cold drafts often seep through cracks in the walls, sometimes dropping temperatures to near freezing. Conversely, some sections trap heat, becoming oppressively warm and humid, increasing exhaustion and dehydration risk for wanderers. ===Chambers=== {{Blurimage ALT|file=Screenshot 2025-11-28 143639.png|caption=An Image of a chamber}} Chambers are a series of relatively small, enclosed rooms scattered sporadically throughout Level 435.These rooms resemble abandoned chambers or small supply closets, typically filled with shelves stocked with a wide variety of supplies. Items include canned food, [[Object 1|almond water]], flashlights, batteries, first aid kits, clothing, and occasionally, more specialized survival gear such as radios, maps, or makeshift weapons. The condition of these supplies varies some are fresh, while others show signs of decay or long-term storage. Unlike the surrounding endless maze of decaying corridors and hostile environments, these rooms possess an unusual quality: entities such as Smilers, Deathmoths, Hounds, and Skin-stealers appear unable or unwilling to enter them. This phenomenon has made Chambers safe havens rare pockets of relative security amid a hostile level The air inside The Chambers is slightly fresher, with less humidity and an absence of the pervasive musty odor found elsewhere in Level 435. Lighting inside these rooms is stable and brighter than the rest of the level, provided by well-functioning overhead lamps or lanterns that never flicker or fail. Due to their rarity and safety, The Chambers have become hotspots for wanderers seeking respite and resources. However, access is unpredictable some appear as if from nowhere, while others may vanish after a short period or after prolonged occupancy. Wanderers are advised to make the most of their time inside, replenishing supplies and resting before continuing their journey. == Entities == A number of entities reside within this level, including: *[[Entity 3|Smilers]] *[[Entity 8|Hounds]] *[[Entity 4|Deathmoths]] *[[Entity 35|Spines]] *[[Entity 10|Skin-Stealers]] *[[Entity 63|Predatory doors]] *[[Entity 36|Lighters]] *[[Entity 62|Aranea Membri]] *[[Sanguine Festivus|Sanguine Festivus/Carriers]] *[[Entity 77|Tunnelis Callidus]] *[[Entity 6|Dullers]] *[[Entity 104|Sentinels]] *[[Entity 64|Cells]] *[[Entity 65|Bloodmist]] ==Colonies and Outposts== Due to the danger of the level, creating outposts or communities is heavily discouraged ==Entrances and Exits== ===Entrances=== *Slipping into a hole on [[Level 8]] has a chance of taking you here *Certain Parts in [[Level 434]] have a chance of taking you here ===Exits=== *Finding a locked metal door and opening it with a special key found in one of the chambers will lead a wanderer to [[Level 217]]. *Noclipping into a wall during a blackout will lead one to [[Achromatism|Level 227]]. *Red doors lead to [[Level 444]]. *Using a Level key to unlock a rare white door in one of the chambers will lead you to a [[Special:RandomInCategory/Levels|random level]] [[Category:Cluster I]] [[Category:Dangerous]] [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Class 5]]
